behavior_summary: U8P_SWORD::look is a process-level handler for the 'U8P_SWORD::look' (examine) action on the sword item. When invoked it emits a single short bark/label "sword " and then suspends the process.
There are no conditionals, menu interactions, parameter inputs, or external API calls in this script; its behavior is purely descriptive.
The script likely serves only to present the short description/label for the item and then pause/terminate the U8P_SWORD::look interaction via suspend.
notable_control_flow: Single straight-line flow: bark (player-facing text) -> suspend. No branches or calls.
Process type 0x0208 marks this as a U8P_SWORD::look/examine handler invoked by the engine when the player examines the item.
open_questions: Does the trailing space in the bark text "sword " carry any gameplay meaning or is it an artifact?
Does the suspend node represent waiting for player dismissal of the description, or a process teardown step handled by the engine?
Are item properties (e.g., overlay.animationStyle 'sword' and usedBy weapon lists) present in the item metadata expected to be consumed elsewhere by the engine?

main_function.name: U8P_SWORD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown when the player examines the sword item: output the label "sword " and then suspend/end the U8P_SWORD::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs a 'U8P_SWORD::look' (examine) action on this item (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_SWORD::look
function_map.role: U8P_SWORD::look/examine handler that emits a short descriptive bark and suspends
